$comment: R2 (D-SEMANTIC-NULL-CONSTANT-FOLDING): a folded-zero integer constant expression `1 - 1` passed to a pointer parameter is a C null pointer constant — it must lower to NULL across every target, not fail loud and not pass garbage. f(p)=p?7:42 is non-inlined so the pointer value flows through param-passing; NULL yields 42, a mis-materialized non-null would yield 7 (42 vs 7, mod-256-safe). The optimizedPipelines arm confirms the semantic-materialized synthetic Literal-0 / Cast-to-Ptr survives the full release pipeline identically (baseline==optimized). Red-on-disable: revert R2 and `f(1 - 1)` fails to COMPILE (S_TypeMismatch).
